topic: An artist discovers someone has tattooed their work
raw_output:
[story 1]

The turpentine fumes were doing nothing for the heavy cloud settling in my chest. I slammed the palette knife down onto the wooden table, the sound sharp and unwelcome in the quiet studio. Another dead-end piece. I stared at the canvas, a landscape of muted blues and bruised purples that refused to breathe. Then I saw it—a line, almost invisible against the texture of the impasto, a delicate, precise etching that wasn't part of my layering. My jaw tightened. I leaned in, squinting, my irritation mounting. It was a pattern, intricate and undeniably someone else's hand, woven right into the flow of my composition. A cold knot formed in my stomach.

[story 2]

My shoulders were hunched, a permanent defensive posture against the unwelcome light filtering through the dusty window. I was trying to focus on the meticulous detail of the portrait, but my concentration kept fracturing. It was the subtle shading around the subject's eye that snagged my attention. It wasn't the brushwork; it was a mark, a dark, fine line that seemed to follow the curve of the iris. I paused, my hand hovering over the brush. A slow, heavy sigh escaped my lips. I traced the mark with a fingertip, the sensation feeling alien and wrong against the oil paint. This was not mine.

[story 3]

I picked up my coffee mug, the ceramic warm against my palms, and took a long, slow sip, letting the bitterness coat my tongue. Everything felt unnecessarily loud today; the city traffic outside sounded like a physical assault. While cleaning brushes, I noticed it—a series of tiny, geometric symbols embedded deep within the background of my abstract piece. They weren't accidental smudges. They were deliberate. A low hum of annoyance vibrated through my fingertips. I set the mug down with unnecessary force, my eyes narrowing as I tried to decipher the foreign script etched into my own creation. Someone had claimed a piece of my vision.
